tags.name: `mc-checkbox`
tags.description: Purpose: Display a checkbox with optional labels, hints, and error messages. Category: Forms, Actions When to use: Forms, Surveys, User preferences When not to use: Decorative purposes, Non-interactive elements Works with components: mc-checkbox-group, mc-card, mc-modal, mc-drawer, mc-notification Alternative component names: Checkbox, Tickbox, Selection Box Events: * `click` {`MouseEvent`} - Fired when mc-checkbox is clicked. * `change` {`CustomEvent`} - Fired when mc-checkbox in checked/unckecked. * `focus` {`FocusEvent`} - Fired when mc-checkbox is focused. * `blur` {`FocusEvent`} - Fired when mc-checkbox is going out of focus. * `input` {`CustomEvent`} - Slots: * ``label`` {} - The label HTML to use for the mc-checkbox. * ``hint`` {} - The hint HTML to use for the mc-checkbox. * ``errormessage`` {} - The errormessage HTML to use for the mc-checkbox. Attributes: * `ariarole` {`AriaRoles | undefined`} - * `fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the checkbox. * `label` {`string`} - Label has to be passed as a property. It is required attribute as it will be used as `aria-label`. * `hint` {`string | undefined`} - Hint can be passed as simple argument like: `hint="hint text"` or as a named slot: `hint text as HTML`. Use argument style for passing short hints, use named slot when you want to pass hint with HTML text. * `hiddenlabel` {`boolean`} - If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value, then the label will not appear, but will be used as `aria-label` only. * `invalid` {`boolean`} - If `true`, checkbox border is red and the errormessage can be displayed if provided. * `errormessage` {`string | undefined`} - Error message will be shown only if `invalid` attribute is set to `true`. Error message can be passed as simple argument like:` errormessage="error"` or as a named slot:` error message as HTML`. Use argument style for passing short messages, use named slot when you want to pass error message with HTML text. * `readonly` {`boolean`} - Sets checkbox to `readonly` state. * `indeterminate` {`boolean`} - Indeterminate state gets precedence over the `checked` state and the checkbox `does not` consider to be `checked` when is in `indeterminate` state. There are not many use cases for this property. The most common is when a checkbox "owns" a number of sub-options (which are also checkboxes). If all of the sub-options are checked, the owning checkbox is also checked, and if they're all unchecked, the owning checkbox is unchecked. If any one or more of the sub-options have a different state than the others, the owning checkbox is in the `indeterminate` state. * `tabindex` {`number | undefined`} - Sets the `tabindex` attribute. * `disabled` {`boolean | undefined`} - Sets checkbox in the `disabled` state. * `name` {`string | undefined`} - Name is required if the component is used inside a `form` element. * `value` {`string | undefined`} - Pass the value for the input if it is used inside a `form` element. * `checked` {`boolean | undefined`} - Sets checkbox in the `checked` state. * `files` {`FileList | undefined`} - * `type` {`string | undefined`} - * `required` {`boolean | undefined`} - Properties: * `input` {`HTMLInputElement | undefined`} - * `focused` {`boolean`} - * `ariarole` {`AriaRoles | undefined`} - * `icon` {`string | undefined`} - * `fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the checkbox. * `label` {`string`} - Label has to be passed as a property. It is required attribute as it will be used as `aria-label`. * `hint` {`string | undefined`} - Hint can be passed as simple argument like: `hint="hint text"` or as a named slot: `hint text as HTML`. Use argument style for passing short hints, use named slot when you want to pass hint with HTML text. * `hiddenlabel` {`boolean`} - If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value, then the label will not appear, but will be used as `aria-label` only. * `invalid` {`boolean`} - If `true`, checkbox border is red and the errormessage can be displayed if provided. * `errormessage` {`string | undefined`} - Error message will be shown only if `invalid` attribute is set to `true`. Error message can be passed as simple argument like:` errormessage="error"` or as a named slot:` error message as HTML`. Use argument style for passing short messages, use named slot when you want to pass error message with HTML text. * `readonly` {`boolean`} - Sets checkbox to `readonly` state. * `indeterminate` {`boolean`} - Indeterminate state gets precedence over the `checked` state and the checkbox `does not` consider to be `checked` when is in `indeterminate` state. There are not many use cases for this property. The most common is when a checkbox "owns" a number of sub-options (which are also checkboxes). If all of the sub-options are checked, the owning checkbox is also checked, and if they're all unchecked, the owning checkbox is unchecked. If any one or more of the sub-options have a different state than the others, the owning checkbox is in the `indeterminate` state. * `tabindex` {`number | undefined`} - Sets the `tabindex` attribute. * `focus` - Sets the focus to the checkbox * `blur` - Takes focus out of the checkbox * `click` - Click on the checkbox * `change` - Fires when the checkbox's checked status is changed. * `disabled` {`boolean | undefined`} - Sets checkbox in the `disabled` state. * `_onMouseDown` - * `cachedValue` {`string | boolean | undefined`} - * `_value` {`string | boolean | undefined`} - * `name` {`string | undefined`} - Name is required if the component is used inside a `form` element. * `value` {`string | undefined`} - Pass the value for the input if it is used inside a `form` element. * `checked` {`boolean | undefined`} - Sets checkbox in the `checked` state. * `files` {`FileList | undefined`} - * `type` {`string | undefined`} - * `required` {`boolean | undefined`} - * `hiddenInput` {`HTMLInputElement | undefined`} - * `controlType` {`string`} - * `isCheckable` {`boolean`} - * `inputType` {`string`} -

attributes.name: indeterminate
attributes.description:
`indeterminate` {`boolean`} - Indeterminate state gets precedence over the `checked` state and the checkbox `does not` consider to be `checked` when is in `indeterminate` state.
There are not many use cases for this property. The most common is when a checkbox "owns" a number of sub-options (which are also checkboxes).
If all of the sub-options are checked, the owning checkbox is also checked, and if they're all unchecked, the owning checkbox is unchecked.
If any one or more of the sub-options have a different state than the others, the owning checkbox is in the `indeterminate` state.

Default: false
